questions.text: Who was the first President of the USA?
In which year did WW2 end?
Who discovered America in 1492?
Which country gifted the Statue of Liberty?
In which city is the Eiffel Tower?
Who was the first woman to fly solo across the Atlantic?
The Great Wall of China was primarily built to protect against which group?
Which empire was ruled by Julius Caesar?
The Renaissance began in which country?
Who wrote the Declaration of Independence?
Which city was the first to be struck by an atomic bomb?
Who was the British Prime Minister during most of WW2?
The Magna Carta was signed in which year?
Who was known as the 'Maid of Orleans'?
The French Revolution began with the storming of which prison?
Who was the first human to travel into space?
The Titanic sank in which ocean?
Which ancient civilization built the Machu Picchu?
Who was the first man to step on the moon?
The Berlin Wall fell in which year?
Which pharaoh's tomb was discovered by Howard Carter in 1922?
The Industrial Revolution began in which country?
Who was the leader of the Soviet Union during the Cuban Missile Crisis?
Which document ended the American Revolutionary War?
Who was the first Emperor of Rome?
The ancient Olympic Games originated in which country?
Which explorer led the first expedition to circumnavigate the globe?
The apartheid system was enforced in which country?
Who was the queen of Ancient Egypt who had a relationship with Julius Caesar?
The Vietnam War ended in which year?
Which US President was responsible for the Emancipation Proclamation?
The Black Death plague occurred during which century?
Who was the primary leader of the Indian Independence Movement?
Which treaty officially ended World War I?
Who was the king of Macedon who created one of the largest empires of the ancient world?
Which obscure treaty signed in 1494 divided the newly discovered lands outside Europe between Portugal and Castile?
Who was the Byzantine emperor who survived the Nika Riots in 532 AD and commissioned the rebuilding of the Hagia Sophia?
Which Chinese dynasty was founded by Zhu Yuanzhang (the Hongwu Emperor) after the overthrow of the Mongol-led Yuan Dynasty?
The Battle of Lepanto in 1571 was fought between the Ottoman Empire and which coalition of Christian maritime states?
Which 18th-century conflict is widely considered the first global war, fought across Europe, the Americas, and India?
options: The War of the Spanish Succession

questions.text: Who was the last ruling monarch of the Kingdom of Hawaii before the monarchy was overthrown in 1893?
Which peace treaty in 1648 recognized the independence of the Dutch Republic and the Swiss Confederation?
Which Roman general defeated Hannibal Barca at the Battle of Zama in 202 BC?
The Meiji Restoration of 1868 returned practical imperial rule to Japan under Emperor Meiji, ending which military government?
Who was the Spanish conquistador who led the conquest of the Inca Empire in Peru?
Which dynastic conflict in England (1455-1487) was fought between the rival houses of Lancaster and York?
Which U.S. President signed the Indian Removal Act of 1830, leading to the forced displacement known as the 'Trail of Tears'?
The Edict of Nantes (1598), which granted substantial rights to Calvinist Protestants (Huguenots) in France, was issued by which king?
Who was the prominent leader of the Haitian Revolution, a former slave who became a general and governor-general?
Which uprising in China (1899-1901) was an anti-foreign, anti-colonial, and anti-Christian movement supported by the Qing court?
Which ancient Greek philosopher and scientist was appointed as the tutor of a young Alexander the Great?
The Han Dynasty of China is widely considered a golden age, but it was briefly interrupted by which usurper's Xin Dynasty?
At which battle in 1815 was the French Emperor Napoleon Bonaparte finally defeated by allied British and Prussian forces?
Which Mongol leader successfully united the nomadic tribes of Northeast Asia and founded the Mongol Empire in 1206?
Which famous speech delivered by Winston Churchill in Fulton, Missouri, in 1946 popularized the term 'Iron Curtain'?
options: We Shall Fight on the Beaches

questions.text: Which crucial conference in February 1945 saw Churchill, Roosevelt, and Stalin discuss the post-war reorganization of Germany and Europe?
The 'Reconquista' was a centuries-long campaign to retake the Iberian Peninsula from Islamic rule, ending with the fall of which last emirate in 1492?
Which absolute monarch of France, known as the 'Sun King' (le Roi Soleil), built the Palace of Versailles and reigned for 72 years?
What was the name of the socio-economic campaign launched by Mao Zedong in China (1958-1962) that aimed to rapidly transform the country from an agrarian economy into a socialist society?
Which explorer commanded the first expedition that successfully sailed from Europe to India around the Cape of Good Hope?
The Peloponnesian War (431-404 BC) was a devastating ancient Greek conflict fought primarily between which two prominent alliances led by rival city-states?
Which Persian emperor founded the Achaemenid Empire and issued the famous Cyrus Cylinder, often called the first declaration of human rights?
Which prime minister of the United Kingdom, serving during the mid-19th century, is known for repealing the Corn Laws and founding the modern Conservative Party?
The ancient city of Carthage, a powerful rival of the Roman Republic in the Punic Wars, was located in what modern-day country?
What was the name of the secret treaty signed in 1916 between the UK and France, with Russian assent, that defined their proposed spheres of influence in the Middle East after WW1?
